root_text.an6.65:1.1: “Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo anāgāmiphalaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.65:1.3: Assaddhiyaṁ, ahirikaṁ, anottappaṁ, kosajjaṁ, muṭṭhassaccaṁ, duppaññataṁ—
root_text.an6.65:1.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme appahāya abhabbo anāgāmiphalaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.65:2.1: Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo anāgāmiphalaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.65:2.3: Assaddhiyaṁ, ahirikaṁ, anottappaṁ, kosajjaṁ, muṭṭhassaccaṁ, duppaññataṁ—
root_text.an6.65:2.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme pahāya bhabbo anāgāmiphalaṁ sacchikātun”ti.

translation_text.an6.65:1.1: “Mendicants, without giving up six things you can’t realize the fruit of non-return.
translation_text.an6.65:1.3: Lack of faith, conscience, and prudence; laziness, unmindfulness, and witlessness.
translation_text.an6.65:1.4: Without giving up these six things you can’t realize the fruit of non-return.
translation_text.an6.65:2.1: After giving up six things you can realize the fruit of non-return.
translation_text.an6.65:2.3: Lack of faith, conscience, and prudence; laziness, unmindfulness, and witlessness.
translation_text.an6.65:2.4: After giving up these six things you can realize the fruit of non-return.”
